acceptance_criteria.summary: bin/cld now defaults to --permission-mode auto and only forwards --dangerously-skip-permissions when the wrapper receives explicit --skip-perms. The explicit opt-in path prints a visible yellow stderr warning containing WARNING and dangerously-skip-permissions.

tests/test_launcher_permission_modes.py covers safe defaults and explicit opt-in for plain passthrough, -b, -bq, and -br. It also stubs bd show with raw untrusted title, description, notes, and acceptance criteria markers and asserts the -b/-bq cld-owned prompt text does not include the marker.

commits.message: fix(CL-w3q3): green use safe cld permission default

tests.command: UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_launcher_permission_modes.py tests/test_cld_single_bead_contract.py
tests.result: Red gate before production change failed as expected: 9 failed, 23 passed. Failures showed default argv still contained --dangerously-skip-permissions and --skip-perms emitted no warning.

tests.command: UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_launcher_permission_modes.py tests/test_cld_single_bead_contract.py
tests.result: Green gate after production change passed: 32 passed.
Passed with no shell syntax errors.

scope_changes.description: Original AC3, bead-orchestrator subagent prompt construction on the cld path, is cross-repo work in cognovis-core and was not attempted in this library-platform bead.

downstream_impacts: bin/cld no longer bypasses Claude permission checks by default for plain passthrough, -b, -bq, or -br invocations.
Users who intentionally pass --skip-perms still get --dangerously-skip-permissions, but now receive a visible stderr warning.
The cld-owned -b and -bq launcher prompt remains limited to bead id, portless namespace, and launcher-derived routing fields; raw bd show fields are not embedded.
No cognovis-core files were edited; original AC3 remains deferred to cognovis-core bead clc-n21h.

evidence_refs.description: The red pytest run against tests/test_launcher_permission_modes.py and tests/test_cld_single_bead_contract.py failed before production code changed because default cld argv still included --dangerously-skip-permissions and opt-in emitted no warning.
The green pytest run passed after bin/cld changed the default branch to --permission-mode auto and added the explicit opt-in warning.

tests/test_cld_single_bead_contract.py::test_cld_bead_review_defaults_to_opus_and_uses_review_only_prompt now asserts -br defaults to --model opus followed by --permission-mode auto and no dangerous flag.

tests/test_launcher_permission_modes.py::test_cld_bead_prompt_does_not_embed_raw_bead_fields stubs bd show with ZZZ_UNTRUSTED_BEAD_FIELD_MARKER_ZZZ in raw bead fields and asserts the marker is absent from -b and -bq prompts.
UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_launcher_permission_modes.py tests/test_cld_single_bead_contract.py returned 32 passed.
